description_long: This object consists of three large, cylindrical metal barrels with a weathered and heavily rusted appearance. The barrels are primarily painted white, but the paint is chipped and worn, revealing extensive patches of orange-brown rust and some remnants of blue paint, especially on the tops. Each barrel features three prominent horizontal ridges, which serve as structural reinforcements. The tops and bottoms are circular, with the tops showing additional rust and faded blue coloration, and no visible openings or lids. The barrels are grouped together, with two standing upright and one lying on its side, all exhibiting significant surface corrosion and signs of long-term outdoor exposure. These barrels are typical of those found in industrial or urban environments, often used for storage or waste.
description: Three large, rusted metal barrels with chipped white and blue paint, grouped together and showing heavy weathering.
description_view_0: Two upright and one horizontal barrel, showing extensive rust and chipped paint, with blue tops visible.
description_view_1: The bottoms of the barrels are visible, with one barrel lying on its side and showing a fully rusted base.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the cylindrical shape, horizontal ridges, and the weathered, rusted surfaces of all barrels.
description_view_3: Rear view of the two upright barrels, with the third barrel partially obscured and more rust detail visible.
